title: Implement synchronized canonical event-state progression across transaction helpers
summary: Implement the Phase 2 runtime changes for v0.4.13 so canonical transaction events, materialized transaction state, and guarded helper behavior remain synchronized throughout ticket execution. Integrated regression coverage now confirms that state_store surfaces append-succeeded/materialization-failed diagnostics to .agent/errors.jsonl, state_rebuilder reports canonical ordering drift and rebuild diagnostics for invalid commit-before-verify sequences, and ops_tools/commit_manager refuse to use drift rebuild output as continuation state while guarded helper behavior stays aligned across the tested boundary.
outputs: Runtime implementation that keeps canonical event append, tx_state materialization, and guarded helper reads synchronized
Precondition enforcement that rejects invalid transaction transitions before further canonical mutation
Safer start/resume behavior that prevents duplicate non-terminal tx.begin and stale-state follow-up actions
acceptance_criteria: After tx.begin is emitted successfully, subsequent task lifecycle helpers observe the same transaction as active without requiring manual recovery or rebuild-driven guessing.
Resume paths bind to the existing non-terminal transaction and do not emit a second tx.begin for the same open ticket.
tx.verify.start, tx.verify.pass, tx.verify.fail, tx.commit.start, tx.commit.done, tx.commit.fail, and tx.end.* transitions materialize the corresponding tx_state before downstream guarded logic runs.
Guarded helper behavior does not disagree about whether a transaction exists, is in progress, is verified, or is terminal.

The implementation rejects specification-violating flows such as lifecycle before tx.begin, duplicate non-terminal tx.begin, commit start before canonical verify pass, and post-terminal non-terminal follow-up events.
If canonical event append succeeds but materialized state cannot be synchronized, the runtime surfaces an integrity failure and stops invalid follow-up progression.
The implementation does not silently replace an ambiguous or inconsistent active transaction with a default none state during normal runtime progression.
tasks: Review current runtime helper entry points and canonical event mutation paths to identify where event append, tx_state persistence, and guard reads can diverge.
Implement explicit runtime enforcement for valid transaction progression so helper behavior follows the v0.4.13 state-machine contract rather than distributed assumptions.
Repair start versus resume flow selection so existing non-terminal work is resumed canonically instead of re-begun.

Ensure event append and tx_state materialization remain coupled strongly enough that downstream verify, commit, and lifecycle helpers cannot observe stale state after successful canonical transitions.
Add integrity handling that stops follow-up helper execution when synchronized canonical state cannot be established after an attempted transition.
Document any helper-boundary constraints or invariants in code comments where ordering and synchronization are critical to transaction correctness.
notes: This ticket is the main Phase 2 runtime implementation ticket for 0.4.13.
Do not weaken workflow guards to mask invalid transaction flows; fix the underlying canonical progression and synchronization behavior.
Treat resume misclassification, duplicate begin, and stale-state verify or commit decisions as one class of transaction-correctness failure.
Prefer failing early with explicit integrity context over allowing later helper calls to cascade into secondary guard errors.
Keep runtime behavior aligned with the explicit state-machine and forbidden-transition rules defined in docs/draft_0.4.13.md.
